thesis: Batch 3 splits leveraged and unleveraged GLD-defensive paths and tests explicit risk scaling through position size so the campaign can decide whether the new TQQQ-driven winners are attractive after de-risking.

benchmark.purpose: Primary passive benchmark for all campaign batches

universe.name: core_us_macro_v2
universe.selection_rule: Focused GLD-defensive exploit universe with separate leveraged and unleveraged risk tracks

carry_forward_findings: GLD-defensive rotation is the dominant current theme.
TQQQ/GLD variants now need a dedicated high-risk track.
QQQ/VOO/TLT remains only a legacy control candidate.
GLD and TQQQ trend timing are both worth refining with explicit sizing.

candidates.hypothesis: VOO buy-and-hold is required for the risk-scaling batch.
GLD buy-and-hold is required for the risk-scaling batch.
TQQQ buy-and-hold is required for the risk-scaling batch.
QQQ buy-and-hold is required for the risk-scaling batch.
QQQ/VOO/GLD 126/7 remains the non-leveraged rotation control.
TQQQ/VOO/GLD 126/7 remains the leveraged rotation control.
QQQ/VOO/TLT 126/21 remains the legacy defensive control.
GLD 20/120 + 189-day time stop is the active GLD trend control.
TQQQ 20/120 + 189-day time stop is the leveraged trend control.
QQQ 40/180 + 252-day time stop is the aggressive non-leveraged trend control.
